page: Hetzner Cloud cax21 is an ARM64-based virtual server powered by the Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 4 virtual CPUs consisting of 4 physical cores with 1 thread per core running at 2.0 GHz under a QEMU hypervisor. The CPU allocation is shared. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 1.95 GB of memory per core, and includes 80 GB of local SSD storage. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate moderate performance characteristics. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests yield average results of 1805.19 and 7235.85, respectively. However, memory performance is mixed; while large block read-only and write bandwidths achieve average ratings at 38.95 GB/sec and 25.37 GB/sec, the passmark memory score is poor at 1087.43. Cryptographic and integer performance is limited, as evidenced by a poor passmark CPU encryption score of 646.59. Static web serving throughput reaches 4.75 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations perform at 5.16 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the cax21 offers a highly economical entry point into ARM64 computing, trading off peak performance for low resource costs. Given the shared CPU allocation and weak-to-average benchmark scores in compiling, text processing, and LLM inference, this server is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, development environments, basic web serving, and testing ARM64-native applications.
description: Hetzner Cloud cax21 is an ARM64 virtual server powered by an Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 4 shared vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 80 GB of local SSD storage, with no integrated GPU. Benchmark results place the server's CPU performance in the average tier, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 7235.85, though memory and cryptographic performance are relatively weak. The instance offers a highly cost-efficient profile for users seeking ARM64 architecture without high resource overhead. It is best suited for lightweight workloads, including basic web serving, development and testing environments, and running ARM64-native applications that do not require dedicated CPU resources.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cax21 is an ARM64 virtual server featuring 4 shared Ampere Altra vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 80 GB of local SSD storage, offering a cost-effective platform for lightweight ARM64 workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cax21 is an ARM64 virtual server with 4 shared Ampere Altra vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 80 GB of SSD storage for budget-friendly workloads.
tagline: An economical ARM64 virtual server with shared Ampere Altra cores and local SSD storage for lightweight development and testing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4-core Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor at 2.0 GHz
Equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM and 80 GB of local SSD storage
Shared CPU allocation under a QEMU hypervisor environment
Average multi-core CPU performance with weaker memory and cryptographic speeds
Highly cost-efficient option for ARM64-native software development and testing
Suitable for lightweight web serving and basic database operations
